title: CRM Software Cost Comparison for 2026
description: A transparent comparison of CRM software pricing across major platforms, including Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive, and Zoho CRM.
body:
CRM software is a critical investment for sales and marketing teams, but pricing varies dramatically across platforms and tiers. Choosing the right CRM requires understanding not just the sticker price but what each tier includes and where costs scale as your team grows. Pricing for SaaS tools changes periodically, so checking each vendor's current pricing page for the latest figures is recommended before making a purchasing decision.

This guide compares the leading CRM platforms across their pricing tiers to help you budget accurately.

## Salesforce Pricing

Salesforce remains the most widely adopted enterprise CRM platform. Its pricing is structured across several suites designed for different team sizes and needs. The Starter Suite is priced at $25 per user per month and includes core sales and service features for small teams. The Pro Suite at $100 per user per month adds pipeline management, forecasting, and workflow automation. The Enterprise plan at $175 per user per month provides advanced analytics, AI-powered insights, and extensive customization. The Unlimited plan at $350 per user per month includes unlimited support, full API access, and sandbox environments. For organizations deploying AI-powered sales agents, the Agentforce 1 Sales tier is priced at $550 per user per month, adding autonomous prospecting and lead engagement capabilities.

Salesforce is best suited for organizations with dedicated CRM administration resources and complex sales processes. Its pricing reflects its extensive feature set and customization capabilities, but total cost of ownership includes implementation services and ongoing administration.

## HubSpot Pricing

HubSpot offers a free CRM tier that includes contact management, deal tracking, and email integration for up to 1 million contacts. The Starter tier at $15 per month for up to 1,000 contacts adds pipeline automation and meeting scheduling. The Professional tier at $800 per month for up to 2,000 contacts includes sequences, custom reporting, and team management. The Enterprise tier at $3,600 per month for up to 10,000 contacts adds custom objects, predictive lead scoring, and multiple currencies.

HubSpot's pricing structure works well for small to mid-sized teams that want a unified platform for marketing, sales, and service. The free tier is generous enough for many small businesses to use indefinitely, and the upgrade path is clear as needs grow.

## Pipedrive Pricing

Pipedrive offers a free tier with basic deal and contact management. Paid tiers start at the Starter level and scale through Pro and Enterprise plans. Pipedrive's pricing is focused on sales pipeline management and is generally positioned below enterprise CRM platforms. Current pricing for each tier is available on Pipedrive's pricing page.

## Zoho CRM Pricing

Zoho CRM offers a free tier with lead and contact management for up to a limited number of users. Paid tiers include Standard, Professional, and Enterprise plans with progressively advanced features including workflow automation, AI assistant, and multi-user portal capabilities. Detailed current pricing is published on Zoho CRM's pricing page.

## Choosing Based on Budget

For small teams with limited budgets, HubSpot's free tier or Zoho CRM's free tier provide solid foundations without upfront investment. Growth-stage teams should evaluate HubSpot's Starter tier or the entry-level paid tiers of Pipedrive or Zoho CRM. Mid-market organizations with dedicated sales operations teams will find Salesforce's Pro Suite or HubSpot's Professional tier appropriate. Enterprise organizations should budget for Salesforce's Enterprise or Unlimited plans, or HubSpot's Enterprise tier, depending on their ecosystem and integration requirements.
author: PilotStack Team
